verses.text: David asks who may dwell with God on His holy hill, and answers: the one who walks uprightly, does what is right, and 'speaketh the truth in his heart.' Fellowship with God requires integrity. Outward worship without an honest heart cannot abide in His presence.

David asks who may dwell with God on His holy hill, and answers: the one who walks uprightly, does what is right, and 'speaketh the truth in his heart.' Fellowship with God requires integrity. Outward worship without an honest heart cannot abide in His presence.

Such a one slanders no one, keeps his word even to his own loss, lends without usury, and takes no bribe against the innocent; he shall never be moved. True religion is proved in daily dealings. The life that honours God in small things stands secure.
